key_items: US inflation tops 4% creating macro headwinds for Bitcoin
Bitcoin jumps above $62,000 after CPI report but $12B leaves network signaling capitulation
Traditional finance institutions rushing into crypto to buy the dip despite TradFi advisers preferring stablecoins and tokenization over Bitcoin
reasoning: Bitcoin shows mixed signals with institutional accumulation competing against macro inflation concerns and on-chain capitulation indicators. The recent jump to $62,000 appears relief-driven rather than conviction-based, with significant capital outflows and PnL indicator still in transition phase suggesting bottom may not be imminent.
risks: Persistent inflation above 4% combined with on-chain capitulation signals and continued Bitcoin ETF outflows ($213.85M net outflow on June 10) could trigger a retest of the $60,000 support level or lower within the 1-7 day timeframe.
